title: Olympus UC1800 Ultrasonic Cleaner - Unit Won’t Start / Power Issue
description: Guide for troubleshooting power issues on the Olympus UC1800 ultrasonic cleaner, focusing on external checks before internal repair.

steps.title: Check Power Source
steps.instructions: Confirm the wall outlet is live by testing with another device (lamp, multimeter, etc.). Verify no GFCI outlets have tripped. Why: A dead outlet or tripped breaker is a common cause of “no power” issues.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Plug
steps.instructions: Check the power cord for cuts, kinks, or burn marks. Ensure the plug is fully seated in the outlet. Why: Damaged cords prevent the device from receiving power safely.

steps.title: Confirm Power Switch Position
steps.instructions: Ensure the main power switch is turned on (some units require pressing and holding). Why: Users sometimes forget the switch position.

steps.title: Check Fuse or Circuit Breaker (if accessible)
steps.instructions: Some UC1800 units have an internal fuse accessible via a fuse holder. Verify the fuse is intact; replace with manufacturer-specified fuse if blown. Why: A blown fuse is a safety measure that prevents device operation.

steps.title: Reset the Unit (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Some models have a reset button or power cycle procedure. Disconnect from power for 1–2 minutes, then reconnect and attempt start. Why: Power interruptions or minor faults can require a reset to clear.

steps.title: Check for Visible Signs of Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the control panel and housing for burn marks, melted components, or liquid ingress. Why: Physical damage may indicate internal fault or unsafe conditions.

CCR.Complaint: UC1800 ultrasonic cleaner won’t power on.
CCR.Cause: Verified outlet is live; power cord intact; fuse intact; unit still unresponsive.
CCR.Resolution: Unit removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent to repair vendor for internal evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Unusual smells or sounds: None/Describe if present
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Olympus UC1800 Ultrasonic Cleaner - Poor Cleaning Performance
description: Guide to troubleshoot poor cleaning performance on the Olympus UC1800 ultrasonic cleaner, focusing on external factors before internal failure.

steps.title: Verify Power and Controls
steps.instructions: Ensure the unit is plugged into a properly grounded outlet. Confirm the power switch is on and the display is functioning. Check that the timer and power settings are correct. Why: The cleaner cannot operate effectively if it’s not receiving full power or is set incorrectly.

steps.title: Check Water Level
steps.instructions: Ensure the water level is above the minimum fill line and below the maximum. Low water reduces ultrasonic cavitation, which diminishes cleaning performance.

steps.title: Inspect Cleaning Solution
steps.instructions: Confirm you are using the correct ultrasonic cleaning solution recommended for the items being cleaned. Replace solution if it is dirty, contaminated, or has been used multiple times. Why: Dirty or inappropriate solutions absorb ultrasonic energy, reducing cleaning effectiveness.

steps.title: Check Water Temperature
steps.instructions: Verify the water temperature is within the recommended range (typically 30–50°C for general cleaning). Cold water can reduce ultrasonic activity, while overly hot water can damage some instruments.

steps.title: Assess Load Size and Arrangement
steps.instructions: Ensure items are not touching each other or the tank walls. Avoid overloading the tank; too many items can block ultrasonic waves. Why: Proper spacing allows uniform cavitation and improves cleaning efficiency.

steps.title: Look for Tank Contamination or Deposits
steps.instructions: Check for scale, debris, or residue on the tank bottom. Clean the tank with a soft cloth and mild detergent if needed.

steps.title: Check for Air Bubbles and Degassing
steps.instructions: When adding fresh solution, run the unit briefly to degas water (usually 2–3 minutes). Air bubbles reduce ultrasonic energy transmission.

steps.title: Test Ultrasonic Function
steps.instructions: Use the “foil test” (placing a small piece of aluminum foil in the tank and running a short cycle). Even pitting on the foil indicates proper ultrasonic activity. Why: Confirms whether the transducers are generating ultrasonic waves correctly.

CCR.Complaint: UC1800 not cleaning instruments effectively; residue remains after standard cycle.
CCR.Cause: Water level low, solution dirty, and items crowded in tank; ultrasonic energy not optimally delivered.
CCR.Resolution: Refilled tank to proper level, replaced solution, rearranged items for spacing, ran degassing cycle; cleaning performance restored.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (functional / out of service)

title: Olympus UC1800 Ultrasonic Cleaner - Error Codes / Sensor Faults
description: Guide for troubleshooting error codes and sensor faults on the Olympus UC1800 ultrasonic cleaner, emphasizing external causes first.

steps.title: Power Check
steps.instructions: Verify the unit is plugged into a working outlet. Check for blown fuses or tripped breakers. Ensure the power switch is ON. Why: No power or unstable power can cause false sensor errors.

steps.title: Water Level and Quality
steps.instructions: Ensure the tank is filled to the minimum and maximum levels. Confirm water is clean; overly dirty solution can trigger error codes. Why: The UC1800 has sensors that detect fluid presence and conductivity.

steps.title: Temperature Sensor Check
steps.instructions: If equipped, confirm the heater sensor is fully immersed in water. Look for bent or disconnected probes. Why: Dry operation or sensor misalignment can trigger error codes.

steps.title: Connection and Cable Inspection
steps.instructions: Inspect all internal and external connectors for corrosion, moisture, or looseness. Verify that any removable sensor connectors are seated properly. Why: Loose or dirty connections are a common cause of sensor faults.

steps.title: Cleaning Cycle Reset
steps.instructions: Power cycle the unit (turn OFF → wait 30 seconds → turn ON). Try starting a basic cleaning cycle without ultrasonic power (if possible). Why: Software glitches may clear with a simple reset.

steps.title: Solution Conductivity
steps.instructions: Ensure the cleaning solution is compatible with the UC1800. Avoid using pure water without proper ultrasonic detergent. Why: Incorrect solution conductivity may trigger sensor fault codes.

CCR.Complaint: UC1800 displays sensor error E02 and will not start cleaning cycle.
CCR.Cause: Observed water level below minimum; heater sensor partially lifted out of solution.
CCR.Resolution: Refilled tank to proper level and ensured sensor fully immersed; cycled power and verified normal operation. If issue persists, unit removed for repair.

helpfulDetails: Water level at time of error
Power supply voltage and outlet check results
Final device status (working, out of service, sent for repair)

title: Olympus VISERA Elite Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Camera Head Not Recognized
description: Identifies causes when the camera head is not detected by the processor, preventing image display and delaying surgical procedures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the camera system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Power to the Processor
steps.instructions:
Ensure the VISERA processor is powered on and all status lights are normal.
A powered-down processor or unstable power can prevent camera detection.

steps.title: Check Camera Head Connection
steps.instructions:
Disconnect and reconnect the camera head from the processor, ensuring proper alignment and full insertion.
Look for bent pins or foreign debris in the connector that may block communication.

steps.title: Inspect Coupler or Adapter (if used)
steps.instructions:
Some VISERA systems use couplers or adapters between the scope and camera head.
Ensure the coupler is seated properly and free of damage.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Camera Head
steps.instructions:
Swap in a verified working camera head to determine if the issue is isolated to the original head.
If the known good head is recognized, the original camera head may be defective.

steps.title: Check Processor Firmware and Settings
steps.instructions:
Confirm the processor firmware is current and the input port configuration matches the connected camera type.
Incorrect settings can prevent recognition even with proper connections.

steps.title: Observe Processor Indicators
steps.instructions:
Processor may display error codes or flashing lights indicating a connection problem.
Note any messages for documentation and escalation.

CCR.Complaint: Camera head not recognized by VISERA Elite processor, no video output.
CCR.Cause: Camera head connection misaligned; coupler seated incorrectly; original head failed recognition test.
CCR.Resolution: Re-seated connections, tested with known good camera head, device removed from service for repair.

helpfulDetails: Error codes or indicator lights noted
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Olympus VISERA Elite Endoscopic / OR Camera System - White Balance Failure
description: Identifies causes of incorrect color, tint, or failed white balance that affect image accuracy during endoscopic procedures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the camera system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Connections
steps.instructions: Check the camera head is properly seated in the processor port. Inspect the cable for damage or loose connectors. Expected Outcome: Secure connections should allow recognition of the camera and enable color settings.

steps.title: Check Processor Settings
steps.instructions: Access the VISERA Elite processor menu and ensure white balance mode is selected. Some procedures may require manual or automatic white balance reset. Expected Outcome: Processor confirms white balance function is active.

steps.title: Clean the Camera Lens and Light Guide
steps.instructions: Wipe the distal lens and light guide with approved lens cleaning solution. Residue or debris can skew color readings. Expected Outcome: Clear lens improves accuracy of white balance.

steps.title: Re-run White Balance Procedure
steps.instructions: Use the processor’s white balance function per standard protocol. Follow prompts exactly; ensure room lighting is adequate and target surface is neutral-colored. Expected Outcome: Color calibration completes without error.

steps.title: Swap Accessories
steps.instructions: If issues persist, try a different camera head or light cable if available. Expected Outcome: Confirms whether the problem lies with the original head or processor.

CCR.Complaint: Endoscopic image colors appear incorrect, unable to complete white balance.
CCR.Cause: Camera head and processor connections verified; manual white balance fails; lens clean.
CCR.Resolution: Reattempted white balance with alternate accessories; issue persists; device removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Olympus VISERA Elite Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Image Artifact or Quality Loss
description: Identifies causes of visual distortions, artifacts, or image degradation that reduce clarity and accuracy during endoscopic procedures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Camera Head and Lens
steps.instructions: Check for smudges, condensation, or debris on the lens or protective cover. Clean with approved lens wipes; image clarity should improve if surface contamination is the cause.

steps.title: Verify Cable and Connector Integrity
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect the camera head cable to the processor. Inspect for bent pins, loose connections, or damaged shielding. A secure connection should restore normal image signal.

steps.title: Check Light Source and Fiber Optic Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the light cable is fully seated and free of kinks. Verify the light source intensity and confirm no flickering occurs. Image artifacts caused by uneven illumination should resolve after correcting the connection.

steps.title: Confirm Display Settings and Processor Output
steps.instructions: Verify white balance, brightness, and contrast settings on the processor. Reset settings to default if necessary. Misconfigured settings can mimic image artifacts.

steps.title: Swap Camera Head or Processor (if available)
steps.instructions: Use a known-good camera head with the processor or a different processor with the same camera head. If artifacts persist on a different combination, the issue may be the remaining component.

steps.title: Observe for Intermittent or Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Look for electromagnetic interference or nearby equipment causing signal noise. Ensure no extreme temperatures or humidity are affecting the optics or electronics.

CCR.Complaint: Surgical team reports blurry image with intermittent visual artifacts.
CCR.Cause: Lens contamination and loose fiber optic connection identified.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned lens and secured light cable; image restored to normal.

title: Olympus VISERA Elite Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Light Source Failure
description: Identifies causes of insufficient, flickering, or no illumination during procedures, ensuring safe visualization for endoscopic surgery.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup light source or camera system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Power Connection
steps.instructions: Check that the light source module is securely plugged into a live outlet. Confirm the power switch is on and indicator lights are illuminated. Expected Outcome: Device powers on; if not, external power issue suspected.

steps.title: Inspect Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: Examine light cable and connectors for damage, kinks, or debris. Ensure secure connection to both the camera head and light source module. Expected Outcome: No visible damage; loose connections can cause light failure.

steps.title: Swap Light Cable
steps.instructions: Use a known good cable to test the light source. Expected Outcome: Light returns if cable is faulty.

steps.title: Check Bulb / LED Module Status
steps.instructions: Verify that the bulb or LED module is correctly seated and not burnt out. Some modules have indicator lights or error codes for failure. Expected Outcome: Replacement needed if module fails self-test or emits no light.

steps.title: Verify Settings on Camera Processor
steps.instructions: Confirm light intensity and mode settings on the VISERA Elite processor. Ensure any dimming or standby settings are not reducing output. Expected Outcome: Correct settings restore expected illumination.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff reported no light from VISERA Elite camera during procedure.
CCR.Cause: Light cable found loose; LED module not fully seated.
CCR.Resolution: Re-seated LED module and secured cable; verified light output restored.

helpfulDetails: Indicator lights on power module observed
Device functional after cable and module check

title: Olympus VISERA Elite Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Scope Connection or Port Damage
description: Identifies causes of faulty scope connections or damaged ports that prevent proper communication between camera head and processor, risking image loss or procedure delays.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup scope or camera system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Scope Connector
steps.instructions: Examine the scope connector for bent pins, debris, or visible damage. A damaged connector can prevent signal transmission. Expected outcome: pins aligned, connector clean.

steps.title: Inspect Camera Port
steps.instructions: Check the processor port for debris, foreign objects, or damage. Damaged ports may prevent recognition of the scope. Expected outcome: port is clean and pins are intact.

steps.title: Check Cable Seating
steps.instructions: Re-seat the scope into the port securely. Sometimes poor seating causes intermittent recognition. Expected outcome: scope recognized by processor; image displays correctly.

steps.title: Test Alternate Scope or Cable
steps.instructions: Swap with a known-good scope or connection cable. This isolates whether the issue is the scope or the port. Expected outcome: known-good scope works, confirming original scope or cable issue.

steps.title: Check System Settings
steps.instructions: Ensure the processor input settings match the connected scope type. Mismatched settings can mimic connection failure. Expected outcome: processor correctly recognizes the scope.

CCR.Complaint: Scope not recognized by VISERA Elite processor during procedure.
CCR.Cause: Bent pins observed on scope connector; processor port appeared intact.
CCR.Resolution: Scope removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for repair; backup scope used for procedure.

helpfulDetails: Inspected for bent pins, debris, or port damage

title: Olympus OTV Series Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Camera Head Not Recognized
description: Identifies causes when the camera head is not detected by the processor, often due to connector incompatibility, preventing image display and delaying procedures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup system before troubleshooting to avoid risk.

steps.title: Inspect the Camera Head Connector
steps.instructions: Check the physical connector for damage, bent pins, or debris. Proper alignment ensures communication between camera head and processor.

steps.title: Verify Connector Compatibility
steps.instructions: OTV Series may require a specific connector variant. Confirm the camera head matches the processor input type. Using the wrong connector prevents recognition.

steps.title: Check Cable and Adapters
steps.instructions: Swap camera cables or any adapters with a known working set. Faulty or incompatible cables can block recognition even if connectors appear correct.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Turn off the camera processor and camera head, wait 30 seconds, then power on. This resets handshake communication between devices.

steps.title: Confirm Processor Settings
steps.instructions: Ensure the processor is configured for the correct camera head type. Some OTV processors require manual selection of input source or camera model.

steps.title: Test With a Known Working Camera Head
steps.instructions: Connect a verified working camera head to confirm the processor port is functional. If the replacement works, the original head may be incompatible or faulty.

CCR.Complaint: Camera head not recognized by OTV Series processor during OR procedure.
CCR.Cause: Camera head connector incompatible with processor input.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced cable with correct connector type; system recognized camera head successfully.

helpfulDetails: Indicator lights on camera and processor
Final device status: operational with correct connector

title: Olympus OTV Series Endoscopic / OR Camera System - White Balance / Color Calibration Differences
description: Addresses inconsistent color reproduction or white balance shifts between procedures, ensuring accurate tissue visualization and reliable surgical imaging.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup system before beginning any checks.

steps.title: Verify Ambient Lighting
steps.instructions: Ensure the OR lights or room lighting have not changed significantly, as this can affect perceived color. Expected outcome: color differences are minimized.

steps.title: Check Camera Head and Coupler
steps.instructions: Inspect for dirt, debris, or moisture on the camera lens or coupler. Clean with approved lens wipes. Outcome: clearer and more consistent color if external contamination was the cause.

steps.title: Confirm Proper Connector Seating
steps.instructions: Ensure the camera head is fully seated in the processor port. Loose connections can cause signal issues affecting color calibration. Outcome: color stabilization.

steps.title: Perform White Balance Procedure
steps.instructions: Execute the system’s white balance routine according to manufacturer instructions, using a proper reference target if required. Outcome: restored color accuracy.

steps.title: Compare with Other Systems
steps.instructions: If available, connect the same camera head to a known working OTV processor. Outcome: determines whether the variation is from the camera head or the processor.

steps.title: Check for Software/Mode Differences
steps.instructions: Verify video output settings, image enhancement modes, and color profiles match between systems. Outcome: eliminates software-induced color differences.

CCR.Complaint: Surgeon reports that tissue colors appear inconsistent between procedures.
CCR.Cause: Camera head lens and processor connections verified; white balance had drifted; ambient lighting and settings differences noted.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned camera head, reseated connections, performed white balance, verified color accuracy against reference processor.

helpfulDetails: Video mode and output settings recorded

title: Olympus OTV Series Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Image Artifact or Quality Loss (Different Processing Algorithm)
description: Addresses visual distortions, graininess, or color artifacts caused by processor algorithm differences, ensuring accurate and consistent surgical imaging.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup system before any troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect the Camera Head and Scope
steps.instructions:
Disconnect and reconnect the camera head to ensure a secure connection.
Inspect scope tip and lens for debris or condensation, which can create artifacts.
Expected outcome: Clear physical connections and clean optics.

steps.title: Verify Video Processing Mode
steps.instructions:
Access the OTV processor menu and check image processing settings (e.g., “Standard,” “High Definition,” or algorithm-specific modes).
Confirm settings match those used in prior procedures.
Why this matters: Different algorithms can alter sharpness, color saturation, or artifact appearance.

steps.title: Swap to a Known Good Camera Head or Scope
steps.instructions:
Connect a different, verified camera head and scope to the same processor.
Expected outcome: If artifacts disappear, the original camera or scope may have sensor or lens issues.

steps.title: Check Video Cables and Connections
steps.instructions:
Inspect HDMI/DVI or proprietary cables for bends, damage, or loose connections.
Replace cables temporarily to see if artifacting resolves.
Expected outcome: Stable, artifact-free image.

steps.title: Confirm Processor Firmware Version
steps.instructions:
Verify the OTV processor firmware matches standard system configuration.
Note any version differences, as algorithm updates may affect image rendering.
Expected outcome: Processor operating with correct firmware reduces algorithm-related artifacts.

CCR.Complaint: Surgical team reports grainy image with color distortions during laparoscopy.
CCR.Cause: Processor set to different image processing algorithm than prior procedures.
CCR.Resolution: Adjusted processor to standard mode and verified image clarity; backup scope tested.

helpfulDetails: Processor video mode and firmware version
Final device status: operational, image quality restored

title: Olympus OTV Series Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Light Source Low Intensity or LED Driver Error
description: Addresses low illumination or LED driver warnings during endoscopic procedures, ensuring safe visualization.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup light source or camera system before beginning any checks.

steps.title: Check Power and Connections
steps.instructions: Verify the system is plugged into a properly grounded, active outlet. Inspect the power cord and connectors for damage or loose connections. Expected outcome: device powers on normally; no power warnings.

steps.title: Inspect Light Source and LED Cables
steps.instructions: Check the light cable for kinks, bends, or frayed ends. Disconnect and reconnect the light cable to ensure proper seating. Expected outcome: low-intensity warnings may clear; LED driver warning may disappear if connection is restored.

steps.title: Verify Settings and Mode
steps.instructions: Confirm that light intensity is set appropriately on the processor. If using multiple light modes, cycle through them to see if intensity improves. Expected outcome: normal brightness at proper settings.

steps.title: Swap with Known Good Components
steps.instructions: Replace the LED light source or light cable with a verified working unit. Expected outcome: intensity returns to normal, indicating a defective component.

steps.title: Check LED Driver Warnings
steps.instructions: Observe if the processor displays specific LED driver errors. If errors persist despite known good cables and light source, internal driver may be failing.

CCR.Complaint: Surgical staff reported low illumination and LED driver warning during laparoscopic case.
CCR.Cause: Light cable and settings verified; LED driver fault suspected after component swap.
CCR.Resolution: System removed from service and sent for repair; backup light source provided for procedure.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: Out of Service

title: Olympus OTV Series Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Scope Port, Coupler, or Processor-Specific Connection Errors
description: Addresses connectivity issues between scope, coupler, and processor that prevent image transmission or system recognition, ensuring reliable visualization during procedures.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is not in use on an active patient. Move the procedure to a backup scope or processor before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Physical Connections
steps.instructions: Check that the scope, coupler, and processor connectors are fully seated and free from debris or damage. Loose or dirty connections often cause errors.

steps.title: Verify Coupler Compatibility
steps.instructions: Ensure the coupler matches the scope and processor model. Using mismatched couplers may prevent proper signal transmission.

steps.title: Swap Cables / Ports
steps.instructions: If multiple ports or cables are available, test with a known good cable or alternate processor port to isolate the faulty component.

steps.title: Check Indicator Lights / Error Messages
steps.instructions: Observe processor and coupler LEDs or on-screen error codes. This helps identify if the issue is port-specific or general connection failure.

steps.title: Restart System Components
steps.instructions: Power cycle the processor, coupler, and scope. Some errors clear after a controlled restart. Confirm proper reconnection after restart.

steps.title: Confirm Firmware / Software Alignment
steps.instructions: Ensure the processor and coupler firmware versions are compatible. Incompatible firmware can cause recognition errors or communication failures.

CCR.Complaint: Processor reports "scope not recognized" during procedure setup.
CCR.Cause: Coupler connection misaligned; no internal processor errors identified externally.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared connections, reseated scope and coupler, error persisted; device sent to repair.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: Out of Service

title: Olympus EVIS EXERA III Endoscopic / OR Camera System - No Image or Video Output on Monitor
description: Troubleshooting no video output on monitor caused by connection, power, input selection, or accessory issues in the imaging chain.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the system is in use, stop the procedure and transition to a backup imaging system.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm there is no image on the monitor and observe screen status, system power, and any error messages.

steps.title: Check Monitor Power and Input Selection (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Verify monitor power is on, cycle input sources, and ensure correct input matches the connected video cable.

steps.title: Inspect Video Cable Connections
steps.instructions: Reseat all video cables, inspect for damage, and confirm secure connections at both ends.

steps.title: Verify Camera Head and Scope Connection
steps.instructions: Reconnect camera head, ensure scope is properly seated, and inspect connectors for damage or contamination.

steps.title: Check Light Source Functionality
steps.instructions: Confirm light source is powered on, brightness is adequate, and illumination is visible at the scope tip.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Power off all components, wait 30 seconds, and restart in sequence: monitor, processor, then light source.

steps.title: Swap Known Good Components (Isolation Step)
steps.instructions: Test with a different monitor, video cable, or camera head to isolate faulty components.

CCR.Complaint: No image displayed on monitor during endoscopy procedure.
CCR.Cause: Loose HDMI cable connection between processor and monitor.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated cable and verified proper input selection; image restored.

helpfulDetails: No unusual sounds, heat, or alarms noted
Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Olympus EVIS EXERA III Endoscopic Camera System - Light Source Not Activating or Low Illumination
description: Light source not turning on, dim output, or inconsistent illumination affecting endoscopic image quality and visibility.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the system is in use, stop the procedure if visualization is compromised. Transition to a backup system if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue and System Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the system and confirm the issue including no activation, dim or fluctuating light, or no illumination at the scope tip. Observe indicator lights, fan noise, and system messages.

steps.title: Check Power to Light Source Unit (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Ensure the unit is plugged into a known good outlet, verify the power switch is ON, and check for status lights or display activity. Test outlet if needed.

steps.title: Inspect Light Cable Connection and Condition
steps.instructions: Confirm light cable is fully seated at both ends, reseat connections, and inspect for burns, cracks, or fiber damage.

steps.title: Check Scope Connection and Compatibility
steps.instructions: Ensure the endoscope is properly connected, verify correct scope usage, and inspect the scope light post for contamination or damage.

steps.title: Verify Light Source Settings / Output Level
steps.instructions: Check brightness settings, ensure output is not at minimum, and adjust intensity to observe response.

steps.title: Inspect Lamp / LED Status Indicators
steps.instructions: Check for lamp life warnings or fault indicators, listen for fan operation, and look for temperature-related errors.

steps.title: Check for Overheating or Ventilation Issues
steps.instructions: Ensure vents are not blocked, verify cooling fans are running, and check for excessive heat.

steps.title: Swap Known Good Components (If Available)
steps.instructions: Try a different light cable, scope, or light source unit to isolate the faulty component.

CCR.Complaint: Light source not activating on Olympus EVIS EXERA III during procedure setup.
CCR.Cause: Loose light cable connection at light source port causing no illumination.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated cable, verified secure connection, and confirmed normal light output restored.

helpfulDetails: Fan operation and heat level checked
Final device status (operational or removed from service)

title: Olympus EVIS EXERA III Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Scope Not Recognized or Communication Error
description: Troubleshooting scope detection and communication errors for Olympus EVIS EXERA III systems caused by connection, compatibility, or interface issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the scope is not recognized during a procedure, stop use immediately and transition to a backup system.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Power on the system and confirm the error message or behavior including scope not recognized message, no image, or failure to initialize.

steps.title: Check Scope-to-Processor Connection (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect the scope, ensure proper seating, alignment, and locking mechanism engagement.

steps.title: Inspect Scope Connector for Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Check for bent pins, moisture, debris, or residue on the connector and port.

steps.title: Confirm Scope Compatibility
steps.instructions: Verify that the scope is compatible with EVIS EXERA III and avoid using incompatible or legacy scopes.

steps.title: Test with a Known Working Scope
steps.instructions: Connect a known-good scope to determine if the issue is with the scope or system.

steps.title: Check System Cables and External Interfaces
steps.instructions: Inspect any intermediate cables or adapters and ensure direct, stable connections.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Turn off the system, wait 30 seconds, and power it back on to clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Observe for Additional Indicators
steps.instructions: Check for error codes, abnormal indicator lights, or irregular startup behavior.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported EVIS EXERA III system not recognizing connected endoscope; no image displayed.
CCR.Cause: Loose scope connector preventing proper communication between scope and processor.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated scope connection and verified proper engagement; system recognized scope and function restored.

helpfulDetails: Whether issue was intermittent or constant

title: Olympus EVIS EXERA III Endoscopic Camera System - System Not Powering On or Random Shutdowns
description: Addresses no power or unexpected shutdowns caused by power supply issues, loose connections, overheating, or external accessory faults.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the system shuts down during a procedure, immediately transition to a backup imaging system. Expected: Patient care continues without interruption Why it matters: Loss of visualization during endoscopy is a critical safety risk

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to power on the system and observe behavior: No response at all (no lights, no fan) Powers on briefly, then shuts down Intermittent shutdown during use Expected: Clear identification of symptom type Why it matters: Helps differentiate power input vs thermal or load-related issues

steps.title: Check AC Power Source (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Verify the outlet is functional using a known-good device Ensure the system is plugged into a proper hospital-grade outlet or power strip Avoid extension cords if present Expected: Stable, verified power source Why it matters: Inconsistent or failed outlets are a common cause of power issues

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Check the AC power cord for damage, fraying, or loose ends Ensure the cord is fully seated at both the wall outlet and system power inlet Swap with a known-good compatible cord if available Expected: Secure, undamaged power connection Why it matters: Loose or faulty cords can cause intermittent shutdowns

steps.title: Check System Power Switch and Indicators
steps.instructions: Confirm the main power switch is functioning properly Observe any indicator lights or front panel status LEDs Listen for fan or internal startup sounds Expected: System shows signs of startup (lights, fan noise) Why it matters: Helps determine if power is reaching internal components

steps.title: Inspect for Overheating Conditions
steps.instructions: Check for blocked vents or dust buildup Ensure adequate clearance around the unit (especially in OR towers) Confirm cooling fans are operating Expected: Proper airflow and normal operating temperature Why it matters: Overheating can trigger automatic shutdown to protect internal components

steps.title: Disconnect External Accessories
steps.instructions: Remove all connected devices (scopes, light source cables, monitors, peripherals) Attempt to power on the base unit alone Expected: System powers on without accessories Why it matters: Faulty peripherals or shorted cables can cause shutdowns

steps.title: Check Power Distribution (If in Equipment Tower)
steps.instructions: Verify the system is not connected to a failing power strip or isolation transformer Try plugging directly into a wall outlet if possible Expected: Stable operation on direct power Why it matters: Tower power systems can introduce intermittent faults

steps.title: Attempt Power Cycle Reset
steps.instructions: Disconnect power completely for 60 seconds Reconnect and attempt startup Expected: System reinitializes normally Why it matters: Clears temporary faults or internal protection states

CCR.Complaint: EVIS EXERA III system would not power on and intermittently shut down during use.
CCR.Cause: Loose AC power cord connection at system inlet causing intermittent power loss.
CCR.Resolution: Secured power connection and verified stable operation through power cycle testing.

helpfulDetails: Power cord condition and swap performed
No unusual heat or odor detected
Final device status (operational or sent for repair)

title: Olympus EVIS EXERA III Endoscopic / OR Camera System - Image Quality Degradation (Noise, Color Issues, Artifacts)
description: Poor image quality including noise, incorrect color balance, flickering, or visual artifacts caused by external components, connections, or setup issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If image quality is compromised during a procedure, notify clinical staff immediately and transition to a backup system if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Image Issue
steps.instructions: Observe the display and confirm the exact symptom: Noise/graininess, Color distortion (too red, blue, or washed out), Flickering or intermittent signal, Lines or artifacts.

steps.title: Check Monitor and Display Settings (Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Verify monitor input source, brightness, contrast, and color settings. Ensure correct input (HDMI/DVI/SDI depending on setup). Reset monitor to default settings if needed.

steps.title: Inspect Video Cable Connections
steps.instructions: Check all video connections between camera control unit (CCU), light source, and monitor. Reseat all cables firmly, inspect for damage, and swap with a known good cable if available.

steps.title: Verify Camera Head and Scope Connection
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect the camera head to the scope and control unit. Ensure proper alignment and secure locking. Check for contamination on optical connectors.

steps.title: Check Light Source and Fiber Optic Cable
steps.instructions: Confirm light source is powered and functioning. Check brightness level settings. Inspect fiber optic cable for breaks or dark spots.

steps.title: Inspect Scope Optics (High Probability Cause)
steps.instructions: Examine the endoscope for debris, fluid, or fogging on the lens. Clean distal tip using approved methods. Check for internal damage.

steps.title: Check White Balance and System Settings
steps.instructions: Perform a white balance using a proper white reference. Ensure correct mode/settings for procedure and reset image settings if needed.

steps.title: Swap Components to Isolate the Issue
steps.instructions: Swap one component at a time with a known good unit including camera head, scope, light cable, and monitor.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Shut down and restart the full system including CCU, light source, and monitor.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff reported poor image quality with noise and incorrect color during endoscopic procedure.
CCR.Cause: Damaged fiber optic light cable causing reduced illumination and image artifacts.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced light cable with known good unit; verified normal image quality and returned system to service.

helpfulDetails: Any visible artifacts or flickering behavior
